MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-18-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Ann Battin, Esq. (510.210.2755; abattin@battinlaw.com) of Battin Law Firm for Plaintiffs.
Simon Mazzola, Esq. (650.403.4802; simon@simonmazzola.com) of The Mazzola Law Office for Defendant.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS MARIXA RAMOS DBA J&D RECYCLING and J&D RECYCLING LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Amend Complaint.

Plaintiffs' "motion for leave to amend complaint" is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP SEC428.50(c).) Defendant's chief claim of prejudice is delay, but the action was filed this year, the first case management conference has yet to be held, no formal discovery has been taken and no trial date set.

Defendant argues that the amendment will be futile, but a proposed amendment's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
